rulings.Case Title: HERRICK CARVER VS. HVH HAYES STREET, LLC, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication [Ccp 437c]
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 21, 2024 line 3. DEFENDANTS' NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is DENIED. Motion for Summary Adjudication is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion for Summary Adjudication is GRANTED as to the cause of action for elder abuse. No triable issues of fact exist, and Defendant is entitled to a judgment as a matter of law. Plaintiff failed to cite any authority for the proposition that the "right of quiet enjoyment" is "real or personal property" of an elder within the meaning of WIC 15610.30 and failed to provide any evidence that the defendant took Plaintiff's right for quiet enjoyment for wrongful use or with intent to defraud. Motion for Summary Adjudication of the cause of action for tenant harassment is DENIED. Triable issues of fact as to the "bad faith" element of SFRO 37.10B exist. Motion for summary adjudication of the cause of action for breach of implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing arising out of rent increase is DENIED. Pursuant to the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing each party to the contract is obligated not to engage in conduct for the purpose of denying to the other the benefits of the contract. Triable issues of fact exist as to the purpose of defendants' "rent increase" conduct. The Court notes that Plaintiff raises an unpled theory of liability in opposition re: Rent Board post-hearing briefs. The Court does not address this theory as the Motion for Summary Judgment/Adjudication is framed by the pleadings. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ANDREW KING VS. VAL-CHRIS INVESTMENTS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 21, 2024 line 4. DEFENDANT VAL-CHRIS INVESTMENTS, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the cause of action for Intentional Infliction of Emotional Distress. Plaintiff does not cite any authority supporting an IIED claim brought by the foreclosed upon owner who did not use the property as his home/abode/dwelling, etc. Demurrer to the causes of action for wrongful eviction and trespass is overruled. Plaintiff sufficiently states all elements of these causes of action, including Plaintiff's right to possession and possession of the property via someone else with no independent contractual possessory rights of their own. E.g. a tenant remains in possession of the property via a subtenant; a person is subject to post-foreclosure eviction if they are the former trustor or a person claiming under such former trustor; but not if they are a tenant. CCP 1161a ; CCP 1161(1); SFRO. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
